macro_summary: Wednesday's session saw AI chip names stabilize after Tuesday's 3.1% Nasdaq rout, with Micron's blowout Q3 print after the close — EPS $25.11 vs $20.71, +21% surprise, and $22B in customer supply commitments per Reuters — providing the catalyst for a semiconductor rebound. SK Hynix surged +12.5% on the Micron read-through and a planned $29.4B US listing (per Reuters, June 25). WTI continued its post-Hormuz slide to $69.54, now 26% below the May peak, as Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic hit post-conflict highs and US-Iran negotiations progressed; the dollar rode higher on Fed rate-hike bets ahead of today's core PCE release.
commodities_note: WTI at $69.54 has fully unwound the Hormuz premium, sitting well below the $80-105 thesis band — the oil-price shock that defined Q2 is no longer the dominant macro input. Gold at $4,010 (-11% monthly) confirms the safe-haven bid is dissipating. US10Y eased to 4.40% (-2% on the session) but remains above the 4.20% threshold for Singapore bank NIM relief. VIX subsided to 18.63 (-4.4%) after Tuesday's spike — still elevated relative to the mid-June 16.2 low, reflecting lingering rate-hike uncertainty.

rcp_state.terms_summary: Oracle 'Project Jupiter' deal — the structural AI-power catalyst behind the +10.7% gap (per 2026-06-04 report). Natural-gas dependency + dilution history are the standing risks.

writeup.headline: BE RCP consolidating — Session 14 of 25; Oracle Jupiter deal the catalyst, no entry until graduation.
writeup.what_to_do: BE is in the RCP consolidation phase: Session 14 of the 25-session gate following the June 3 Oracle Project Jupiter gap. The structural catalyst (Oracle 2.8 GW deal) re-anchored the stock; the old SMA50 is no longer the reset target. Graduation triggers: Path A (SMA50 retest — already fired at session 6) confirms the consolidation floor; Path B (8% band breakout above ~$309.80) is the active path. Failure: 5+ closes below $272.95 AND below post-gap SMA50 within 60 sessions. No entry until graduation. BE is outperforming the AI Power cluster by 20% over the week — the Jupiter deal premium is holding.

writeup.headline: IFX +16.6% extended, hard-blocked — GaN patent loss in China adds headwind.
writeup.what_to_do: IFX is hard-blocked at +16.6% above its 50-day SMA. The China Supreme Court upheld an injunction blocking Infineon's GaN products in mainland China (per Simply Wall St., June 24). While the standing thesis rests on SiC datacenter power (not GaN in China), the legal setback adds noise. PEG of 0.82 on 30.5x forward PE is attractive, but the 0.8:1 R:R and hard extension block keep entry closed. Reassess on a pullback toward the 50-day at €68.22. Outperforming the AI Power cluster by 7.2% over the week — the SiC premium is holding despite the GaN headline.

writeup.headline: AIXA +2.5% above 50-day — tightest SG entry in AI Power cluster, R:R 1.4:1.
writeup.what_to_do: AIXA at €53.34 is only 2.5% above its 50-day SMA after a -11.1% week — the pullback has brought it closer to entry than any other AI Power Infrastructure name. Lagging the cluster by 7.2% over the week. The GaN MOCVD tool monopoly thesis is intact but slow-cycle — order trends matter more than single-session moves. R:R of 1.4:1 to €55.28 resistance needs a wider upside target for entry; the 50-day retest at €52.02 with volume confirmation is the trigger. FCF yield of 3.0% is attractive. Watch for the €52 level.

writeup.headline: NVTS -17% in a week, R:R 1.8:1 — oversold but GaN thesis lacks near-term catalyst.
writeup.what_to_do: NVTS at $18.32 is 11.9% below its 50-day SMA with RSI at 40.5 — the pullback has created a 1.8:1 R:R to $19.79, but the wide-stop R:R of 0.43:1 fails. Lagging the AI Power cluster by 13.1% over the week and 39.2% over the month. The GaN power-chip thesis has no named near-term catalyst and the stock is thinly covered. No entry until either (a) a named hyperscaler GaN design win or (b) a volume-backed reclaim of the 50-day at $20.78 with a R:R above 2.0.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if NVTS fails to reclaim SMA50 ($20.78) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

writeup.headline: PLTR -17.5% below 50-day, RSI 31 — defense thesis intact but 54x PE needs growth delivery.
writeup.what_to_do: PLTR at $113.50 is deeply oversold (RSI 30.8) and 17.5% below its declining SMA50. R:R of 4.4:1 to $163.70 is attractive on paper, and the wide-stop R:R of 2.2:1 clears the ACCUMULATE gate, but the 54x forward PE (130% premium to BigTech cluster) demands growth delivery that the recent tape doesn't confirm. The $10B Army IDIQ framework (ceiling, not firm revenue) and $448M Navy deal are structural backstops. Reassess on a $102.15 support test with volume capitulation — the defense AI thesis is real but the entry needs a cleaner technical signal.

thesis_highlights: WeRide bull case rests on B2B fleet licensing (Uber, Grab, Bosch) + multi-country Robotaxi deployment — evaluate on commercial traction, not autonomy benchmarks vs Waymo/Tesla.
ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

writeup.headline: WRD RSI 30, -23.7% in a month — Uber Zurich robotaxi deal is traction, but ADR risk persists.
writeup.what_to_do: WRD at $5.60 is deeply oversold (RSI 30.0) and 23.1% below its SMA50. R:R of 1.9:1 to $6.68 resistance is borderline. The Uber Zurich robotaxi launch (per 24/7 Wall St., June 23) confirms commercial traction on the B2B fleet-licensing thesis, but revenue conversion at scale is unproven. ADR/China delisting risk is the standing thesis invalidator. Fragility: single catalyst (Uber/Grab partnerships) — no entry until the Zurich deployment shows multi-quarter revenue durability or a second independent leg (e.g., US permit) emerges. Starter sizing only.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if WRD fails to reclaim SMA50 ($7.28) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

R:R of 2.6:1 to $769.47 is above the 2.0 threshold, but wide-stop R:R of 0.92:1 fails gate 8. The MEMS timing displacement thesis (per IA, May 2026) is intact, but at 64x forward PE with PEG of 3.82, the valuation gate is closed. No ACCUMULATE until either a named hyperscaler design win creates a second independent leg or the wide-stop R:R clears 1.5 on further consolidation.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain extended above their 50-day SMAs with declining volume — OCBC is the only name with positive revenue growth (4.9%) and the most attractive PE (13.8x, at the cluster median), while UOB carries negative revenue growth and DBS is the most overbought (RSI 68). The NIM-compression thesis is unchanged: US10Y at 4.40% keeps USD funding costs above the 4.20% relief threshold, and the next MAS review is October 2026. No entry triggers in the cluster — this is a waiting game for the rate cycle to turn.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally intact, tactically extended — awaiting either US10Y break below 4.20% or October MAS review for NIM relief
singapore.key_development: OCBC expanding Indonesia wealth advisory headcount (per Private Banker International, June 23) — the only SG bank actively adding growth capacity while peers consolidate.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster is bifurcated: MU, SK Hynix, and memory-linked names are surging on HBM demand ($22B in MU customer commitments per Reuters), while NVDA, AMD, and INTC remain under pressure from competitive chip news (Qualcomm AI entry, OpenAI-Broadcom Jalapeño) and extreme extensions. SOXX at $601.50 is +12% on the month but the gains are concentrated in memory — NVDA is lagging the cluster by 15.4% over the month. The Micron print resets the narrative: AI memory demand is structurally tight through 2027, but the memory names are too extended to enter and the merchant-GPU names are being repriced on custom-silicon competition.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish on HBM memory, tactically blocked on extension — custom-ASIC competition compressing merchant-GPU premiums
semiconductors.key_development: Micron Q3 beat ($25.11 vs $20.71) + $22B customer commitments (per Reuters, June 24); SK Hynix +12.5% and $29.4B US listing plan — HBM duopoly thesis fully validated.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is the most actionable cluster on the watchlist: MSFT upgraded to ACCUMULATE at RSI 32.5 with a 20% PE discount to peers, while GOOG was downgraded to WATCH on R:R distortion despite the TPU ramp thesis. AMZN remains HOLD with a distorted 0.6:1 sizing R:R. PLTR is deeply oversold (RSI 30.8) but the 130% PE premium keeps it in HOLD. The cluster's thesis divergence is sharpening — MSFT's Azure non-OpenAI RPO is the cleanest demand signal, GOOG's TPU ramp is the best custom-silicon story, and AMZN's $200B capex needs AWS margin proof.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — MSFT's oversold valuation creates entry, GOOG's TPU ramp needs technical confirmation, AMZN awaiting margin delivery
big_tech.key_development: MSFT ACCUMULATE at 18.9x forward PE (20% BigTech discount); OpenAI-Broadcom Jalapeño chip (per The Verge, June 24) validates custom-ASIC as the competitive threat to merchant GPU models.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVDA photonics investment alongside LITE — co-packaged optics for AI datacenter interconnects; the MU/SK Hynix HBM capacity surge directly increases optical-interconnect demand at scale.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's next earnings confirm >$400M AI optics backlog conversion with a named hyperscaler — COHR will re-rate in sympathy.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC competitor to AVGO and merchant-silicon alternative to NVDA — the OpenAI-Broadcom Jalapeño chip validates the custom-ASIC model, which is MRVL's core AI growth thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AVGO's next earnings for custom-ASIC revenue growth trajectory — MRVL trades at a discount to AVGO on the same thesis.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: SK Hynix's US listing plan and Micron's $22B customer commitments highlight the HBM duopoly — Samsung's HBM3E qualification struggles are the structural risk to the duopoly thesis and a potential re-rate if resolved.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung announces HBM3E qualification with NVDA — would break the SK Hynix/Micron duopoly and reprice the entire HBM supply chain.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking backbone — NVDA Spectrum-X and 800G optical rollouts directly increase Arista's switching addressable market; Micron's AI memory boom signals sustained datacenter capex through 2027.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for a pullback to the 200-day moving average — Arista is the highest-quality networking pure-play and benefits from every AI datacenter expansion cycle.

interconnected.name: Qualcomm
interconnected.reason: Entering AI data-center chip market (per Yahoo Finance, June 24) with $15B revenue target by 2029 — competes directly with NVDA's inference business and validates the custom-ASIC trend that AVGO/MRVL are riding.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if QCOM's next earnings quantify AI data-center pipeline — the $15B 2029 target is ambitious and needs quarterly evidence of design-win conversion.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz: tanker traffic at post-conflict highs (109 vessels Sat-Mon per Kpler/Fox Business), but ~$125B in vessels/cargo remain stranded (per Allianz/WSJ) — mines and port damage persist; US-Iran 60-day negotiating window open.

US-China tech decoupling: China retaliated with export controls on 10 US defense firms (per BBC, June 23); Alibaba sues Pentagon over 1260H blacklist; MATCH Act threatens ASML DUV/EUV exports to China — Dutch Trade Minister opposing in Washington (per Zamin.uz, June 25).

Fed hawkish repricing: BofA calls for 75bps of 2026 rate hikes (per Reuters, June 24); core PCE release today is the next catalyst — a hot print hardens the Warsh-era hawkish path and pressures duration-sensitive AI capex names.

US manufacturing mixed signals: June S&P Global Manufacturing PMI rose to 55.7 (beat), but factory employment tumbled to a six-year low (per mezha.net, June 23) — front-loading of orders masking underlying demand weakness.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz premium has fully unwound — WTI at $69.54 is 26% below the May peak, and the oil-shock thesis is no longer the dominant input for the watchlist. The AI capex cycle is now the primary driver: Micron's $22B customer commitments confirm AI demand is structural, not cyclical, but Fed hawkishness (core PCE today) pressures the duration-sensitive names (NVDA -7.5% on the month, MSFT -12.7%). Singapore banks remain trapped in the NIM-compression trade with US10Y above 4.20%.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Micron Q3 beat ($25.11 vs $20.71) and $22B customer supply commitments (per Reuters, June 24) — the strongest AI-demand signal since NVDA's Q4 guide. SK Hynix announced a $29.4B US listing (per Reuters, June 25). Core PCE release today (June 25) — the most important inflation print of the quarter for the Fed's next move.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds with WTI near $70 as US-Iran talks progress; core PCE comes in at or slightly above consensus, keeping the Fed on hold but hawkish. AI capex cycle continues — MU's $22B commitments confirm demand — but semis cluster remains capped below 50-day averages for merchant-GPU names while memory names extend further. MSFT and oversold BigTech offer the best risk/reward entries.

optimistic.description: US-Iran permanent peace deal signed, WTI drops below $65 removing the last energy premium. Core PCE cools to 3.8% or below, easing Fed hawkishness and triggering a broad AI relief rally. Semis cluster re-rates as rate-cut odds return — NVDA and ASML reclaim 50-day averages. Singapore banks benefit from US10Y dropping toward 4.00%, reopening the NIM-relief trade.

pessimistic.description: Core PCE prints above 4.5%, BofA's 75bps hike call hardens into consensus. US10Y spikes above 4.60%, crushing duration-sensitive AI capex names. SOXX drops below $550 as the AI-premium unwind accelerates. Hormuz negotiations stall — WTI rebounds to $80+. MSFT and AMZN break below structural supports; watchlist shifts to full defense.

wildcard.description: A major AI chip export control announcement — MATCH Act passes or China blocks InP/gallium substrate exports — reshapes the semiconductor supply chain overnight. ASML, LITE, AXTI, and memory names gap on supply disruption. Custom-ASIC names (AVGO, MRVL) benefit as hyperscalers accelerate in-house silicon to bypass merchant-GPU supply risks.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Deploy a starter MSFT position at $355-368 with half sizing — the R:R is 4.7:1 and the 20% BigTech discount prices in the OpenAI overhang. Keep the rest in cash; 10 of 28 names are hard-blocked on extension, and today's core PCE release is a binary that cuts both ways.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously constructive — one ACCUMULATE signal in a sea of CAUTION
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 10 of 28 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — the semis memory surge (MU +35.5%, SK Hynix +55.3%) is the dominant extension driver. Two RCP gates have failed (INTC, TSEM). The only actionable entry is MSFT at a deeply oversold RSI of 32.5 with a 20% BigTech valuation discount — the rest of the watchlist waits for pullbacks or catalyst clears.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Core PCE ≤ 3.8%, easing Fed hawkishness — US10Y drops toward 4.20%, rate-hike odds fall
bullish_upgrades: MSFT → BUY candidate (rate relief + oversold bounce)
NVDA → ACCUMULATE candidate (wide-stop R:R clears on SMA200 recovery)
D05_SI/O39_SI → WATCH (US10Y approaching NIM relief threshold)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Core PCE ≥ 4.5%, BofA 75bps hike call hardens — US10Y spikes above 4.60%, AI capex duration trade crushed
bearish_impact: MSFT → remains ACCUMULATE but invalidation at $355.51 at risk (extension block reasserts on rate selloff)
NVDA → deeper WATCH (SMA200 test at $190 becomes base case)
SG banks → deeper CAUTION (NIM compression worsens with rising USD funding costs)

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Payrolls < 150k, unemployment ticks up to 4.4% — labor market softening gives Fed cover to hold, easing rate-hike pressure
bullish_upgrades: GOOG → BUY candidate (rate sensitivity reduced, TPU thesis clears technical gate)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Payrolls > 220k, unemployment drops to 4.2% — labor market resilience locks in BofA's 75bps call, US10Y surges
bearish_impact: AMZN → CAUTION (labor costs + rate pressure on capex-heavy name)
CRWV → CAUTION (debt-financed GPU fleet hit by rising rates)
MSFT → CAUTION if $355.51 breaks (rate-sensitive duration trade intensifies)

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: TSM guides Q3 revenue above consensus, CoWoS capacity expansion accelerated to 130k+ wafers/mo — AI demand structurally confirmed
bullish_upgrades: NVDA → ACCUMULATE candidate (TSM capacity = NVDA Blackwell Ultra volume signal)
ASML → WATCH (TSM capex = ASML order signal, MATCH Act risk unchanged)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: TSM guides flat or below, cites customer inventory build or CoWoS demand plateau — AI semis thesis cracks
bearish_impact: NVDA → CAUTION (TSM is the supply signal; flat guide implies Blackwell demand concerns)
ASML → deeper CAUTION (TSM capex cut = direct ASML revenue headwind)
MU → CAUTION (HBM demand linked to GPU packaging capacity; TSM CoWoS plateau hits memory directly)

accumulate_paper_status.line: ACCUMULATE — paper phase: 8/10 matured, -1.8% alpha, trend_up. Not yet validated; size as paper.

first_pass_critical_violations: GOOG: risk_reward.tight_upside=true but writeup.tight_upside_disclosure is missing/empty. Per WRITEUP STRUCTURE conditional fields, emit one sentence citing nearest_resistance ($X.XX, Y% above) and the conditional break-on-volume language.

macro_context_line: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +172k (eased from +179k); unemployment 4.3% (flat, stale); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). Reconcile the rate lean in the macro_summary against this when a CPI/FOMC/jobs event is near.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=0) / ACCUMULATE (n=5) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
